title: Create app/services/tier_classifier.py — tier classification service
context: Tasks need model_tier set before the first workflow node runs. Currently it is never set at creation, so spawn_agent gets no tier and falls back to defaults. Design doc: docs/tier-classification-session-cleanup-design.md. Use llm_direct.py (already exists at app/orchestrator/llm_direct.py) to call local qwen for classification — it handles LM Studio unavailability gracefully.

spec.logic: See design doc. Three-step: (1) caller override passthrough, (2) project minimum floor, (3) LLM via llm_direct, (4) default standard.
spec.llm_prompt: Classify model tier. Title: {title}, Project: {project_id}, Agent: {agent}, Notes: {notes[:400]}. small=boilerplate/docs/scripts/simple fixes, standard=production features/real bugs/integrations, strong=architecture/security/system design. Respond JSON: {"tier": "small|standard|strong", "reason": "one sentence"}
spec.error_handling: Never raise. Any LLM failure or invalid response → return None from inner function → caller defaults to standard

acceptance: classify_task_tier never raises; LM Studio unavailable → 'standard'; lobs-server project + LLM returns 'small' → 'standard'; caller passes 'strong' → 'strong'; invalid LLM response → 'standard'. Unit tests in tests/test_tier_classifier.py.

title: Wire tier classification into app/routers/tasks.py create_task()
context: After the tier_classifier service exists (previous handoff), wire it into task creation. Design doc: docs/tier-classification-session-cleanup-design.md.

spec.location: In create_task(), after db.add(db_task) and before db.flush()
spec.schema_check: Verify TaskCreate in app/schemas.py has model_tier: Optional[str] = None. If missing, add it.

acceptance: POST /api/tasks with no model_tier returns task with non-null model_tier. POST with explicit model_tier='strong' preserves it. lobs-server project tasks get at least 'standard'. Integration test added to tests/test_tasks.py.
